list.name: Changes
list.comment:
Update Media Effect Enhancement to 16.0.0-beta04
This commit updates the `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` dependency version in the version catalog.
Key changes: - **Dependency Update**: Bumped `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` from `16.0.0-beta03` to `16.0.0-beta04` in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`.

project.component: { "keyToString": { "Android App.app.executor": "Run", "Android Instrumented Tests.EnhancementViewModelBenchmarkTest.executor": "Run", "ApkExportedModule": "ImageSuperResolution.app", "Gradle.Configure Daemon JVM Criteria.executor": "Run", "Gradle.Download Sources.executor": "Run", "GradleDaemonJvmCriteriaMigrationNotification.isNotificationDisabled": "true", "ModuleVcsDetector.initialDetectionPerformed": "true", "RunOnceActivity.ShowReadmeOnStart": "true", "RunOnceActivity.TerminalTabsStorage.copyFrom.TerminalArrangementManager.252": "true", "RunOnceActivity.cidr.known.project.marker": "true", "RunOnceActivity.git.unshallow": "true", "RunOnceActivity.readMode.enableVisualFormatting": "true", "SHARE_PROJECT_CONFIGURATION_FILES": "true", "cf.first.check.clang-format": "false", "cidr.known.project.marker": "true", "com.google.services.firebase.aqiPopupShown": "true", "git-widget-placeholder": "benchmark-automated", "kotlin-language-version-configured": "true", "settings.editor.selected.configurable": "editor.preferences.fonts.default" } }

task.summary:
Migrate to Play Services Media Effect Enhancement and implement on-demand module management
This commit migrates the image enhancement implementation from a local library to the Google Play Services (GMS) `play-services-media-effect-enhancement` library. It introduces a robust workflow for checking device support, handling on-demand module installation, and managing the lifecycle of enhancement sessions.
The key changes include: - **GMS Library Integration**: Updated dependencies to use `play-services-media-effect-enhancement`. The `EnhancementUtils.kt` file has been overhauled to wrap GMS Tasks in coroutines using `suspendCancellableCoroutine`, providing a more idiomatic way to handle session creation and bitmap processing. - **Dynamic Module Installation**: Implemented logic in `EnhancementViewModel` to check if the required enhancement module is installed. It now supports downloading the module on-demand with progress tracking, ensuring the app remains functional even if the ML features are not pre-installed. - **Improved Session Management**: Refactored the enhancement logic to use `EnhancementSession`. Sessions are created asynchronously and properly released when a new image is selected or the ViewModel is cleared, preventing memory leaks and resource contention. - **Enhanced UI Feedback**: The `ImageComparisonScreen` now displays the module status (e.g., "Checking support," "Installing") and provides a `LinearProgressIndicator` during the initial download. Specific error messages are now surface-level for both installation and processing failures. - **Native Library & Manifest Updates**: Added required `uses-native-library` entries for OpenCL and GLES to the manifest to ensure compatibility across different hardware architectures (including Pixel and Automotive). - **Tooling Updates**: Updated the Kotlin version and moved the JVM toolchain to version 17 to align with modern development standards.

Implement job cancellation and UI controls for image enhancement
This commit introduces the ability for users to cancel ongoing image enhancement operations and improves task management within the ViewModel to prevent race conditions.
The key changes include: - **Active Job Tracking**: The `EnhancementViewModel` now maintains a reference to the `activeJob` for image selection and processing tasks. This ensures that selecting a new image automatically cancels any previous, unfinished operations. - **Manual Cancellation Logic**: Added a `cancelOperation` function to the ViewModel. It explicitly cancels the current coroutine job, releases the `EnhancementSession`, and resets the UI state to a non-loading, clean state. - **Context-Aware Action Button**: The UI in `ImageComparisonScreen` now dynamically toggles between an "AI Enhance" button and a "Cancel" button based on the current loading state. The "Cancel" button uses the error color scheme to provide a clear visual cue for stopping the process. - **Robust State Management**: Refined the loading logic to ensure the UI remains responsive and consistent when operations are interrupted or canceled.

Implement job cancellation and UI controls for image enhancement
This commit introduces the ability for users to cancel ongoing image enhancement operations and improves task management within the ViewModel to prevent race conditions.
The key changes include: - **Active Job Tracking**: The `EnhancementViewModel` now maintains a reference to the `activeJob` for image selection and processing tasks. This ensures that selecting a new image automatically cancels any previous, unfinished operations. - **Manual Cancellation Logic**: Added a `cancelOperation` function to the ViewModel. It explicitly cancels the current coroutine job, releases the `EnhancementSession`, and resets the UI state to a non-loading, clean state. - **Context-Aware Action Button**: The UI in `ImageComparisonScreen` now dynamically toggles between an "AI Enhance" button and a "Cancel" button based on the current loading state. The "Cancel" button uses the error color scheme to provide a clear visual cue for stopping the process. - **Robust State Management**: Refined the loading logic to ensure the UI remains responsive and consistent when operations are interrupted or canceled.

Integrate BenchmarkTracker for performance monitoring
This commit integrates the `BenchmarkTracker` utility into the image enhancement workflow to measure and log performance metrics across the session lifecycle.
Key changes include: - **Performance Instrumentation**: Added tracking points in `EnhancementViewModel` to record timestamps for session creation, bitmap processing, and resource release. - **Metadata Tracking**: Enhanced the benchmarking logic to capture input/output resolutions and selected enhancement options, providing context for the latency metrics. - **Reporting Logic**: Updated `BenchmarkTracker` to expose the `printSummary` function and automatically clear previous data when a new session begins. - **Lifecycle Hooks**: Integrated tracking for cancellation and session destruction within `EnhancementUtils` and the ViewModel's `onCleared` and `cancelOperation` methods. - **Device Support**: Updated IDE configuration to include support for Pixel Tablet (API 36) in device streaming.

Refactor image enhancement to use callback-based SDK APIs
This commit transitions the image enhancement logic from a suspend-based wrapper to the underlying callback-based API provided by the enhancement library. This ensures more direct integration with the SDK's lifecycle and processing model.
The key changes include: - **Callback-driven Processing**: Refactored `processImage` to use `EnhancementCallback`. The UI state updates for the enhanced bitmap and loading status are now handled within the `onBitmapProcessed` callback. - **Asynchronous Session Creation**: Introduced `createSessionCallback`, which utilizes `EnhancementSessionCallback` to manage the asynchronous creation of an `EnhancementSession`. - **Explicit Session Release**: Added a `releaseSession` function to allow for explicit cleanup of the enhancement session and related UI state. - **Enhanced Lifecycle Management**: Updated `cancelOperation` and `onImageSelected` to ensure sessions are properly released and benchmark metrics are recorded accurately during cancellations. - **Improved Benchmarking**: Refined the placement of `BenchmarkTracker` calls to more accurately capture session creation overhead and pure enhancement latency. - **Logging and Debugging**: Added targeted logging for session lifecycle events (creation, destruction, and errors) to aid in debugging processing issues.

Update Media Effect Enhancement to 16.0.0-beta04
This commit updates the `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` dependency version in the version catalog.
Key changes: - **Dependency Update**: Bumped `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` from `16.0.0-beta03` to `16.0.0-beta04` in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`.

MESSAGE.value:
Migrate to Play Services Media Effect Enhancement and implement on-demand module management
This commit migrates the image enhancement implementation from a local library to the Google Play Services (GMS) `play-services-media-effect-enhancement` library. It introduces a robust workflow for checking device support, handling on-demand module installation, and managing the lifecycle of enhancement sessions.
The key changes include: - **GMS Library Integration**: Updated dependencies to use `play-services-media-effect-enhancement`. The `EnhancementUtils.kt` file has been overhauled to wrap GMS Tasks in coroutines using `suspendCancellableCoroutine`, providing a more idiomatic way to handle session creation and bitmap processing. - **Dynamic Module Installation**: Implemented logic in `EnhancementViewModel` to check if the required enhancement module is installed. It now supports downloading the module on-demand with progress tracking, ensuring the app remains functional even if the ML features are not pre-installed. - **Improved Session Management**: Refactored the enhancement logic to use `EnhancementSession`. Sessions are created asynchronously and properly released when a new image is selected or the ViewModel is cleared, preventing memory leaks and resource contention. - **Enhanced UI Feedback**: The `ImageComparisonScreen` now displays the module status (e.g., "Checking support," "Installing") and provides a `LinearProgressIndicator` during the initial download. Specific error messages are now surface-level for both installation and processing failures. - **Native Library & Manifest Updates**: Added required `uses-native-library` entries for OpenCL and GLES to the manifest to ensure compatibility across different hardware architectures (including Pixel and Automotive). - **Tooling Updates**: Updated the Kotlin version and moved the JVM toolchain to version 17 to align with modern development standards.

Implement job cancellation and UI controls for image enhancement
This commit introduces the ability for users to cancel ongoing image enhancement operations and improves task management within the ViewModel to prevent race conditions.
The key changes include: - **Active Job Tracking**: The `EnhancementViewModel` now maintains a reference to the `activeJob` for image selection and processing tasks. This ensures that selecting a new image automatically cancels any previous, unfinished operations. - **Manual Cancellation Logic**: Added a `cancelOperation` function to the ViewModel. It explicitly cancels the current coroutine job, releases the `EnhancementSession`, and resets the UI state to a non-loading, clean state. - **Context-Aware Action Button**: The UI in `ImageComparisonScreen` now dynamically toggles between an "AI Enhance" button and a "Cancel" button based on the current loading state. The "Cancel" button uses the error color scheme to provide a clear visual cue for stopping the process. - **Robust State Management**: Refined the loading logic to ensure the UI remains responsive and consistent when operations are interrupted or canceled.

Integrate BenchmarkTracker for performance monitoring
This commit integrates the `BenchmarkTracker` utility into the image enhancement workflow to measure and log performance metrics across the session lifecycle.
Key changes include: - **Performance Instrumentation**: Added tracking points in `EnhancementViewModel` to record timestamps for session creation, bitmap processing, and resource release. - **Metadata Tracking**: Enhanced the benchmarking logic to capture input/output resolutions and selected enhancement options, providing context for the latency metrics. - **Reporting Logic**: Updated `BenchmarkTracker` to expose the `printSummary` function and automatically clear previous data when a new session begins. - **Lifecycle Hooks**: Integrated tracking for cancellation and session destruction within `EnhancementUtils` and the ViewModel's `onCleared` and `cancelOperation` methods. - **Device Support**: Updated IDE configuration to include support for Pixel Tablet (API 36) in device streaming.

Refactor image enhancement to use callback-based SDK APIs
This commit transitions the image enhancement logic from a suspend-based wrapper to the underlying callback-based API provided by the enhancement library. This ensures more direct integration with the SDK's lifecycle and processing model.
The key changes include: - **Callback-driven Processing**: Refactored `processImage` to use `EnhancementCallback`. The UI state updates for the enhanced bitmap and loading status are now handled within the `onBitmapProcessed` callback. - **Asynchronous Session Creation**: Introduced `createSessionCallback`, which utilizes `EnhancementSessionCallback` to manage the asynchronous creation of an `EnhancementSession`. - **Explicit Session Release**: Added a `releaseSession` function to allow for explicit cleanup of the enhancement session and related UI state. - **Enhanced Lifecycle Management**: Updated `cancelOperation` and `onImageSelected` to ensure sessions are properly released and benchmark metrics are recorded accurately during cancellations. - **Improved Benchmarking**: Refined the placement of `BenchmarkTracker` calls to more accurately capture session creation overhead and pure enhancement latency. - **Logging and Debugging**: Added targeted logging for session lifecycle events (creation, destruction, and errors) to aid in debugging processing issues.

Update Media Effect Enhancement to 16.0.0-beta04
This commit updates the `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` dependency version in the version catalog.
Key changes: - **Dependency Update**: Bumped `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` from `16.0.0-beta03` to `16.0.0-beta04` in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value:
Update Media Effect Enhancement to 16.0.0-beta04
This commit updates the `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` dependency version in the version catalog.
Key changes: - **Dependency Update**: Bumped `playServicesMediaEffectEnhancement` from `16.0.0-beta03` to `16.0.0-beta04` in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`.
